title: Tag Input Item
description: An array-family variable widget rendering a freeform tag/chip entry field. It binds one document/container variable (the `variable` it sets) whose declared type is `array`; a viewer types arbitrary values that accumulate as tags, and on change the tag list flows through the resolver's override path as an array and re-resolves the document so dependent `${var}`/`$var` consumers update live. A tag-input may only bind a variable whose declared type is `array` — the resolver enforces this widget↔variable type compatibility and reports WIDGET_TYPE_MISMATCH otherwise. Unlike the option-constrained array widgets (`multiselect`, `checkbox-group`), a tag-input is freeform: it declares no option set, so the resolver never requires one. The variable itself is declared in the document/container `variables`, which supplies the effective default; this config only declares the binding and its presentation. A tag-input never has children.

constraints.description: The human label rendered beside the control — editable at runtime.
When true the control is rendered read-only — editable at runtime to gate the input.
Placeholder text shown in the empty control — editable at runtime.

variable.description: Name of the variable this widget sets. Must be a variable visible in the widget's scope (declared at document scope or on an ancestor container) and declared with a type the widget's family permits (array family → array). The tags entered here become that variable's runtime override on re-resolve.

label.description: Optional label rendered above or beside the control.

description.description: Optional help text describing what the input controls, rendered beneath or beside the control.

disabled.description: When true the control is rendered read-only and cannot be changed.

placeholder.description: Optional placeholder text shown in the empty entry field.
